_comment: Single source of truth for safety thresholds. Imported by rules.ts and rendered into lib/ai/system-prompt.ts. Edit here, not in prose.

globalRules._comment: City of Richmond / JRPS rules that apply across all water-contact locations. Source verified 2026-06-02 against jamesriverpark.org/park-rules-safety/ and jamesriverpark.org/riversafety/.
globalRules._pfdSource: https://jamesriverpark.org/park-rules-safety/ — 'Lifejackets must be worn when the River is at or above 5 feet.'

bridge_crossing._comment: Potterfield Memorial Bridge deck overtops at ~25 ft Westham per USACE/NWS HEC-RAS model (elev_123_8 layer in Richmond-Westham FIM). Tile-probe + Chrome UI validation 2026-06-03 (docs/jrps-flood-inundation-thresholds-2026-06-03.md). Bridge deck is ~20 ft above the river per JRPS source; Hurricane Agnes 1972 record crest (~26 ft Westham) is one of the few historical events that overtopped it.

_locationsNote: locations.* notes are ≤60-char family-trip flavor lines. Validated 2026-06-02 via deep-research workflow (docs/jrps-research-2026-06-02.md). Most per-location flood thresholds in the original seed (south_channel_close_ft 5.0, flood_close_ft 10.0) were unsourced and have been removed; the 5.0 ft value is now globalRules.pfdRequiredAboveGageFt — a global PFD trigger, not a per-location closure.

belle-isle._southChannelComment: South-channel rock crossing fully submerged at ~20 ft Westham per USACE/NWS HEC-RAS model (elev_118_8 layer). Practical impassibility is lower due to current speed — do NOT treat this as a 'crossing-safe' threshold; it is a 'rocks fully under water' anchor. Tile-probe + Chrome UI validation 2026-06-03.
belle-isle.notes: Rocky urban island, bike skills + Class II views.

pony-pasture.notes: Class II rapids, swim beach, easy gravel paths.
pony-pasture._comment: flood_close_ft 14.0 is user-provided operational threshold (2026-06-03) — local knowledge, not extracted from the HEC-RAS model. NOAA-published 16 ft Westham is when water enters the parking lot specifically; access becomes problematic earlier (the user-provided 14 ft). The original seed beach_submerge_ft 6.0 was unsourced and is removed.

texas-beach.notes: Secluded swim/wade access via Texas Ave woods.

browns-island._comment: Riverbank inundation begins at the lowest modeled Westham stage (11 ft) per USACE/NWS HEC-RAS model (elev_109_8). Potterfield Bridge deck overtops at ~25 ft Westham per same model (elev_123_8). Tile-probe + Chrome UI validation 2026-06-03.

mayo-island._comment: Unpublished as of 2026-06-02 (locations.published=false). Capital Region Land Conservancy acquired 2022 for future public-park development.
mayo-island.notes: Limited public access — verify before visiting.

shiplock-trail._comment: flood_close_ft 16.0 is user-provided operational threshold (2026-06-03) — local knowledge for the Canal Walk walkway near Great Shiplock Park. Westham FIM doesn't cover this location (eastern boundary is ~Mayo Island); a fully model-derived value would require querying the separate City Locks FIM (gauge 02037705) and translating between gauges. The user-provided 16 ft anchors the Westham gauge to the walkway's practical access cutoff.
shiplock-trail.notes: Canal Walk east of downtown, near river locks.

buttermilk-trail.notes: Hardest JRPS trail — rocks, roots, fast bikes.

pump-house._comment: Building access is via Friends of Pump House monthly guided tours (March–November). Full restoration is a $10M+ multi-year project. Park trails (Pump House Park + adjacent Dogwood Dell) are open year-round.
pump-house.notes: Park trails + monthly tours Mar–Nov. Restoration ongoing.
